name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description: GenAI Client is a Java library designed for seamless integration with Generative AI providers. It offers foundational prompt management and embedding capabilities, enabling AI-powered features across Machai modules. The library simplifies interactions with AI services, supporting advanced use cases such as semantic search, automated content generation, and intelligent project assembly within the Machanism ecosystem. It provides provider-agnostic integration layer used by Machai applications to interact with large language models and related AI services without coupling application code to a specific vendor SDK, authentication flow, endpoint format, or execution model. It centralizes provider resolution, model selection, prompt and instruction collection, optional host-side function tools, embedding requests, and token-usage reporting.

usageContext: Java applications that need provider-neutral access to large language models
Machai modules that execute prompts, collect instructions, request embeddings, and report usage
Applications that expose controlled local Java methods as AI-accessible function tools
Request-scoped AI workflows requiring configurable credentials, base URLs, model names, output limits, and tool-call limits
Applications requiring OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic Claude, or EPAM CodeMie integrations
Systems that need token-usage tracking and reporting for operational visibility
Applications using ServiceLoader-based function tool discovery
Model-assisted workflows with tool-enabled conversational AI providers

constructors.description: Dynamically loads and initializes a Genai provider based on the specified provider/model string. The provider name and model are parsed from the input string (format: Provider:Model). The provider class is resolved using a conventional naming pattern (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.impl.{Provider}Provider) or as a fully qualified class name when the provider segment contains a dot. The provider is instantiated and initialized with the specified model and configuration. Examples of model identifiers: OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini, Claude:claude-3-5-sonnet, CodeMie:<model>, or a fully qualified provider class name.

examples.description: Resolves an OpenAI-backed provider for a single request, supplies instructions and a prompt, runs the model, and records token usage. Replace the null Configurator with the application configuration that provides credentials, endpoints, limits, and model settings.

constructors.description: Dynamically loads and initializes an EmbeddingProvider based on the specified provider/model string. The provider name and model are parsed from the input string (format: Provider:Model). The provider class is resolved using a conventional naming pattern or as a fully qualified class name. The provider is instantiated and initialized with the specified model and configuration.

examples.description: Creates an embedding provider with application configuration and generates a vector suitable for semantic search or similarity matching.

constructors.description: Obtains the singleton loader that discovers FunctionTools implementations through Java ServiceLoader and applies configured host capabilities to providers that support function or tool calling. The loader uses ServiceLoader mechanism to automatically discover and load function tool implementations registered in META-INF/services.

examples.description: Discovers service-loaded tool contributors, configures the loader, creates a Claude provider, and installs available tools before prompts are executed.

features.name: Genai.perform
features.description: Executes the accumulated request for a provider after instructions, prompts, files, URLs, logged inputs, and optional function tools have been registered. Concrete providers map this common call to their backend execution model and return the generated response text. This is the main execution method for generative AI requests.

examples.description: Shows the standard request-scoped workflow: resolve a provider, add instructions and a prompt, and invoke perform to obtain generated text.

features.name: EmbeddingProvider.embedding
features.description: Requests vector embeddings from an AI embedding provider. The embedding contract supports semantic search and embedding-powered workflows when the selected backend supports embedding generation. Returns a list of floating-point numbers representing the vector embedding.

examples.description: Creates an embedding provider with application configuration and generates a vector suitable for semantic search or similarity matching.

features.name: UsageStatistics.addUsage
features.signature: static void addUsage(java.lang.String model, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage usage)
features.description: Adds immutable token usage metrics from a provider interaction to the manager-level aggregate registry. Usage reporting helps track total input, cached input, and output token consumption across multiple requests grouped by model identifier. This is essential for monitoring and cost tracking in production environments.

examples.description: After a provider request completes, capture the provider usage object and log aggregate token statistics for monitoring.

features.name: FunctionToolsLoader.applyTools
features.description: Applies discovered FunctionTools implementations to a provider. Tool registration is separate from provider creation so applications can expose local capabilities only for requests that need them and only for providers that support tool execution. This enables controlled access to host-side functions and resources.

examples.description: Configures the FunctionToolsLoader, installs host tools into an OpenAI provider, and executes a prompt that may invoke those tools.

features.name: Genai.instructions
features.description: Adds system-level instructions to the provider. These instructions guide the AI model's behavior and establish the context for subsequent prompts. Instructions are processed before user prompts and help shape the model's responses.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai provider = null;
provider.instructions("You are a helpful assistant for repository analysis.");
examples.description: Sets system instructions that define the AI assistant's role and behavior.

features.name: Genai.prompt
features.description: Adds a user prompt to the provider. Multiple prompts can be accumulated before calling perform(). The prompt represents the user's question or request to the AI model.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai provider = null;
provider.prompt("Summarize the main features of this library.");
examples.description: Adds a user prompt asking for a summary of library features.

features.name: Genai.clear
features.description: Resets accumulated conversation input between requests when reusing a provider instance. Clears all previously added instructions, prompts, files, URLs, and logged inputs. Usage tracking is not affected by this operation.

examples.description: Clears the provider state after completing a request, preparing it for reuse with fresh context.

features.name: UsageStatistics.logUsage
features.description: Logs the aggregated token usage statistics for all provider interactions grouped by model identifier. This provides operational visibility into AI service consumption and helps with cost tracking and optimization.

examples.snippet: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.UsageStatistics.logUsage();
examples.description: Outputs aggregated token usage statistics to the logging system.

features.name: Genai.init
features.description: Initializes the provider with runtime configuration and a model identifier. Configuration values may contain placeholders such as ${...} which must remain available for runtime substitution. Common settings include credentials, base URLs, request timeouts, output-token limits, and tool-call limits.

examples.description: Initializes an OpenAI provider instance with a model identifier and application configuration.

features.name: Genai.usage
features.description: Returns immutable token-usage metrics for the most recent provider interaction. Captures input tokens, cached input tokens, and output tokens consumed during the request. This information is essential for monitoring, cost tracking, and optimization.

examples.description: Retrieves usage metrics after executing a provider request.

customizations.name: FunctionTools
customizations.description: Service-provider interface (SPI) for installing host-provided function tools. Implementations are discovered with Java ServiceLoader and can expose controlled host capabilities to compatible AI providers. This allows applications to extend the AI model's capabilities by providing access to local functions, APIs, or resources.

examples.description: Step-by-step ServiceLoader scenario for adding host-defined tools to model requests. This demonstrates how to create a custom function tool, register it via ServiceLoader, and apply it to a provider.

customizations.name: Tool annotation
customizations.description: Annotation to mark a method as a tool function within the AI provider framework. Tool methods can be invoked by the AI model during request execution to access host-side capabilities. The annotation requires a description that explains the tool's purpose to the AI model.

examples.description: Marks a method as an AI-accessible tool with a descriptive annotation.

customizations.name: Prompt annotation
customizations.description: Annotation to mark a method as a prompt within the AI provider framework. Prompt methods generate text that can be used as system instructions or user prompts. The annotation supports role specification (SYSTEM, USER, ASSISTANT) to control how the generated text is interpreted.

examples.description: Defines a prompt-generating method with a specific conversation role.

customizations.name: Param annotation
customizations.description: Annotation to mark a method parameter as a Tool or Prompt parameter within the AI provider framework. Provides metadata about the parameter including its description, whether it is required, and enumeration constraints.

customizations.name: SupportedFor annotation
customizations.description: Indicates which application classes a FunctionTools implementation supports. This allows conditional tool loading based on the runtime application context, enabling different tool sets for different applications or environments.

customizations.name: Resource annotation
customizations.description: Annotation used to declare a method as an executable resource provider within the generative-AI tools system. Resource methods map configuration schemas, system guidelines, or instructional assets to one or more URIs, allowing the AI provider to dynamically load context documents.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai
studs.description: Contract for a generative-AI provider integration. It defines initialization, instruction and prompt accumulation, file and URL handling, embedding-related operations where supported, tool registration, execution, usage inspection, and cleanup operations. This is the primary interface that all provider implementations must implement.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider
studs.description: EmbeddingProvider defines the contract for AI embedding providers that produce vector representations for input text and support semantic search or similarity workflows. Implementations generate numerical vector embeddings that can be used for similarity comparisons and semantic search operations.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: Service-provider interface (SPI) for installing host-provided function tools. Implementations are discovered through Java ServiceLoader and applied to compatible Genai providers. This interface allows applications to extend the AI model's capabilities with custom host-side functions.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction
studs.description: Functional interface representing a tool callable by a provider during a run. Tool functions receive structured JSON invocation parameters, the current project directory, and configuration data, allowing them to execute host-side operations on behalf of the AI model.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.AbstractAIProvider
studs.description: Base implementation of the Genai contract shared by concrete provider integrations. Provides reusable base behavior for configuration-driven providers, including timeout handling, request logging, optional web-search support, MCP server registration, annotation-driven tool and prompt discovery, guarded tool invocation with configurable error handling, and reflective method invocation for tool and prompt callbacks.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.GenaiAdapter
studs.description: Delegating Genai implementation that enables wrapper, adapter, and decorator patterns around a concrete Genai instance. This allows for flexible composition and extension of provider functionality.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.impl.OpenAIProvider
studs.description: OpenAI-backed Genai implementation that adapts the OpenAI Java SDK Responses API and embedding API. Supports conversational prompting, function tools, MCP tools, web search, usage tracking, and embedding generation for OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.impl.AnthropicProvider
studs.description: Anthropic-backed implementation of Machai's Genai abstraction. Uses the Anthropic Java SDK Beta Messages API and supports message construction, local function tools, optional web search (versions 20250305 and 20260209), MCP server forwarding, prompt-cache control for large tool results, and usage tracking.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.impl.CodeMieProvider
studs.description: Genai implementation that integrates with EPAM CodeMie. Obtains OAuth 2.0 access tokens using password or client-credentials authentication, and delegates requests to an OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible provider based on the configured model family. Model prefixes gpt-*, gemini-*, text-embedding-*, codemie-text-embedding-*, and amazon.titan-embed-text-* route to OpenAIProvider; claude-* prefixes route to AnthropicProvider.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.impl.ToolsProvider
studs.description: AI provider implementation for managing and invoking host-defined function tools. Executes locally registered function tools directly from structured YAML prompts, which is useful for tool-only workflows and deterministic host-side execution. Operates in a fail-fast mode where exceptions from tool execution are propagated immediately rather than being returned as model text.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage
studs.description: Immutable token-usage metrics for a single generative AI interaction. Captures input tokens, cached input tokens, and output tokens consumed during a provider request.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.UsageStatistics
studs.description: Central registry for aggregated GenAI token-usage statistics. Collects and maintains cumulative usage metrics across multiple provider interactions for monitoring and reporting purposes.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager
studs.description: Utility class for dynamically loading and initializing generative AI providers and embedding providers. Resolves identifiers such as OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini into concrete provider classes using a conventional package pattern or fully qualified class names.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader
studs.description: Discovers and applies FunctionTools implementations using Java's ServiceLoader mechanism. The loader is a singleton that manages the discovery, configuration, and application of function tools to compatible providers.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ParamDescriptor
studs.description: Descriptor for a parameter used in tool or prompt definitions within the AI provider framework. Provides programmatic metadata representation for tool and prompt parameters including name, data type, required flag, description, and default value.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Role
studs.description: Enumeration representing participant roles in an AI interaction context. Defines ASSISTANT and USER roles for organizing conversation messages.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.TypeConverter
studs.description: Utility class for converting between Java types and their string representations, as well as mapping Java types to simplified type names (e.g., \"string\", \"integer\", \"array\"). Used for tool parameter type handling and serialization. Performs runtime conversion of string inputs to typed Java objects including collections, maps, primitives, and types with single-argument string constructors.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.SpecialException
studs.description: Exception used to signal the end of a task without terminating the application. Provides framework-level control flow for graceful task completion in interactive mode.

examples.title: Install and use GenAI Client with Maven
steps: Add the Maven dependency to your project's pom.xml file
Configure runtime settings such as API keys, base URLs, timeouts, and model identifiers
Execute the request with perform() and handle the response
Optionally record usage metrics for monitoring
examples.snippet:
<!-- Add to pom.xml -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.machanism.machai</groupId>
  <artifactId>genai-client</artifactId>
  <version>1.2.2-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

// Java code:
org.machanism.macha.core.commons.config.Configurator conf = null; // Load your configuration
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai provider = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", conf);
provider.instructions("You are a helpful assistant for repository analysis.");
provider.prompt("Summarize the public API of this project.");
java.lang.String answer = provider.perform();
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.UsageStatistics.addUsage("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", provider.usage());
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.UsageStatistics.logUsage();
examples.description: Complete example showing how to add GenAI Client as a Maven dependency, configure it with your settings, create a provider instance, execute a prompt with system instructions, and track token usage. This is the most common usage pattern for integrating the library into a Java application.

examples.title: Create and use custom function tools with ServiceLoader
steps: Implement the FunctionTools interface in your application
Annotate methods with @Tool and parameters with @Param
Use FunctionToolsLoader to discover and apply tools to a provider
Execute prompts that can invoke your custom tools
examples.description: Demonstrates how to extend the AI model's capabilities with custom host-side function tools. The AI model can call these tools during execution to access local resources, APIs, or perform domain-specific operations. This example shows the complete workflow from implementing the FunctionTools interface to using the tools in a provider request.

examples.title: Generate embeddings for semantic search
steps: Configure an embedding provider with model identifier
Call the embedding() method with input text
Use the returned vector for similarity comparisons or search indexing
examples.description: Shows how to generate vector embeddings for text using the EmbeddingProvider interface. Embeddings are useful for semantic search, similarity matching, and building vector databases. This example demonstrates the simple API for generating embeddings that can be used in AI-powered search and recommendation systems.

examples.title: Track token usage across multiple requests
steps: Record usage for each request with UsageStatistics.addUsage
examples.description: Demonstrates how to track and aggregate token usage across multiple AI provider requests. This is essential for monitoring costs, optimizing model usage, and maintaining operational visibility in production environments. The example shows how usage metrics are collected after each request and logged in aggregate.
